task_summary: Fix 5 architectural gaps in the video-editor effect system: (1) Consolidate three effect sources (draft/localStorage, legacy `effects` DB table, newer `resources` table) into a clear migration path — the current code silently merges all three in useEffectRegistry with no dedup or priority. (2) Make the global singleton DynamicEffectRegistry reactive — it's plain mutable state with no React notification mechanism, so components don't re-render when effects register async. (3) Add a runtime error boundary around custom effect rendering — compilation errors are caught but runtime throws from AI-generated effects crash the entire Remotion composition. (4) Make EffectCreatorPanel preview composition match timeline rendering — preview uses hardcoded 320x320/30fps with direct EffectComponent wrapping vs timeline's wrapWithClipEffects/EffectLayerSequence pipeline. (5) Add runtime validation of effect params against parameterSchema — params are typed as `Record<string, unknown>` with no validation at apply-time, so missing/wrong params silently break effects.

key_evidence.point: Three sources merge in useEffectRegistry.ts:39-47 via Promise.all with no dedup — draft, DB (slug-keyed), and resource (UUID-keyed) effects all call registry.registerAsync independently. Last-to-resolve wins for name collisions.

DynamicEffectRegistry is a plain class with no reactive signals. replaceEffectRegistry() swaps the module-level singleton but nothing notifies React components. Registration is async (useEffect + Promise.all) so effects may not be available when Remotion first renders clips.

No ErrorBoundary exists anywhere in the video-editor compositions directory. CompileErrorEffect handles compilation failures, but a runtime throw from a custom effect's render function will crash the entire Remotion Player/composition. ToolErrorBoundary exists in shared/ but is page-level, not per-effect.
key_evidence.source: src/tools/video-editor/effects/compileEffect.tsx:20-39 + Grep for ErrorBoundary in video-editor found 0 results

key_evidence.point: EffectCreatorPanel preview (makePreviewComposition) directly renders <EffectComponent> with hardcoded 320x320 size and PREVIEW_FPS=30, passing effectParams directly. Timeline rendering uses wrapWithClipEffects which wraps continuous→entrance→exit in sequence and gets params from clip data. Preview has no entrance/exit layering.

EffectComponentProps.params is typed as `Record<string, unknown>` with no runtime validation. wrapWithClipEffects passes clip.continuous?.params, clip.entrance?.params, clip.exit?.params directly — if a parameter is missing or wrong type, the effect component receives undefined. getDefaultValues in ParameterControls.tsx exists but is only used in EffectCreatorPanel preview, never at timeline apply-time.

Legacy effects table (useEffects.ts) queries by user_id from `effects` table with slug-based keying. Resource-based effects (useEffectResources.ts) use UUID-based keying from `resources` table with `custom:{resourceId}` prefix in clips. CustomEffectEditor.tsx still uses the legacy useEffects hook directly for editing.
DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx has only 2 tests: built-in preference over collisions, and compile error wrapping. No tests for reactivity, async registration timing, or runtime error handling.

Built-in effects (entrances.tsx) accept params? optional but never use it — only effectFrames, intensity. Custom effects are the ones that use params, and they receive the unvalidated Record<string, unknown> directly.

relevant_code.why: Central hook merging 3 sources — needs dedup/priority logic and reactivity
Global singleton class — needs reactive notification mechanism (useSyncExternalStore or event emitter)
Module-level singleton, lookupEffect, wrapWithClipEffects — all callsites for effect resolution and apply-time wrapping
Compilation pipeline — CompileErrorEffect handles compile errors but no runtime boundary
Preview composition diverges from timeline rendering — makePreviewComposition vs wrapWithClipEffects
Has getDefaultValues/getFallbackValue — reuse at apply-time for param validation
Calls wrapWithClipEffects — needs error boundary wrapping
Calls wrapWithClipEffects — needs error boundary wrapping
Renders continuous effects on track level — needs error boundary wrapping
ParameterSchema, ParameterDefinition types, ClipEntrance/ClipExit/ClipContinuous with untyped params
Legacy DB effects hook — candidate for deprecation
Newer resource-based effects — the canonical path forward
Draft effects in localStorage — third source that merges silently
Wires up all three hooks and passes to useEffectRegistry
Still uses legacy useEffects hook directly — needs migration
Existing tests — extend for reactivity and error boundary behavior
Existing error boundary pattern to reference for the per-effect boundary

test_expectations.what_it_checks: Built-in effects preferred over dynamic collisions; compile errors wrapped not thrown
Deduplication by resource ID and grouping by category

constraints: Built-in effects must always take priority over dynamic effects with the same name (existing invariant in DynamicEffectRegistry.get)
wrapWithClipEffects is called from VisualClip and TextClip — any changes to its signature or behavior affect all clip rendering
EffectLayerSequence wraps all tracks below it — error boundary must not swallow the children when the effect fails, just pass them through unwrapped
Remotion compositions run in a special context (useCurrentFrame, useVideoConfig) — error boundaries must work within Remotion's Player component
CustomEffectEditor.tsx still uses legacy useEffects hook — migration must not break existing saved effects that reference slugs
Clips store custom effect types as 'custom:{resourceId}' — the normalizeName/resolveEffectName stripping must continue to work
ParameterControls.getDefaultValues is used in EffectCreatorPanel and must remain compatible if reused at apply-time
The effects table and resources table coexist — cannot drop legacy table without data migration
suggested_approach:
**Concern 1 — Three sources merged silently:** Add explicit priority in useEffectRegistry: resources > DB > drafts. Log when a DB effect is shadowed by a resource. Add a `@deprecated` JSDoc to useEffects.ts and add a console.warn when DB effects are loaded that says "Legacy effects table is deprecated, migrate to resources." Migrate CustomEffectEditor.tsx to use useEffectResources. Don't remove the DB path yet — just make it clearly secondary.

**Concern 2 — Non-reactive singleton:** Add useSyncExternalStore support to DynamicEffectRegistry. Add a private `version` counter + `subscribe(listener)` method + `getSnapshot()` that returns version. Create a `useEffectRegistryReactive()` hook that calls useSyncExternalStore so components re-render when effects finish registering. Replace bare `getEffectRegistry()` calls in lookupEffect with the reactive version or pass registry from React context.

**Concern 3 — No runtime error boundary:** Create an `EffectErrorBoundary` class component (similar to ToolErrorBoundary but lightweight) that catches runtime throws from effect render. On error: log the effect name + error, render children unwrapped (graceful degradation — show the clip without the effect). Wrap the effect component call in wrapWithClipEffects and EffectLayerSequence with this boundary. Also wrap the preview in EffectCreatorPanel.

**Concern 4 — Preview divergence:** Refactor makePreviewComposition to use wrapWithClipEffects internally by constructing a synthetic ResolvedTimelineClip with the preview's category, params, intensity, and duration. This ensures the same entrance→continuous→exit wrapping order and same prop passing. Keep the 320x320 Player wrapper but use the real effect pipeline inside.

**Concern 5 — Untyped params:** Create a `fillDefaultParams(params: Record<string, unknown> | undefined, schema: ParameterSchema): Record<string, unknown>` utility that merges user params over schema defaults (reusing getFallbackValue logic). Call it in wrapWithClipEffects before passing params to each effect component. Also call it in EffectLayerSequence. This requires that parameterSchema is accessible at render time — store it alongside the effect in DynamicEffectRegistry (extend the `dynamic` record to include `schema?: ParameterSchema`). Register schema from useEffectResources data. For built-ins, schema is empty (they don't use params).
